cards.front: How do you form a polite request from a vowel-ending stem?
cards.grammarNote: Vowel-ending stems take 세요 directly: 마시다 → 마시세요, 기다리다 → 기다리세요. This is the standard polite imperative used in restaurants and public settings.

cards.front: How do you form a polite request from a consonant-ending stem?
cards.grammarNote: Consonant-ending stems take 으세요: 앉다 → 앉으세요, 읽다 → 읽으세요. The 으 bridges the final consonant.

cards.front: Please eat (honorific — to an elder or guest)
cards.grammarNote: 드세요 is the honorific imperative of 먹다. Use 드세요 (not 먹으세요) when offering food to elders or showing respect. 드시다 is the honorific form of 먹다/마시다.
주세요 (from 주다, to give) is the standard way to order food or request items. Add the food item + 주세요. It literally means 'please give me [item].'
